topic: Agentic AI mitigates LLM hallucinations in academic literature reviews.
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews has the potential to mitigate Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations. According to Dwivedi et al. (2023), generative conversational AI presents both opportunities and challenges for research, practice, and policy. The use of Agentic AI can help to address some of these challenges, including the issue of hallucinations (Dwivedi et al., 2023). 

Hallucinations in LLMs refer to the generation of text that is not based on actual facts or evidence (Dwivedi et al., 2023). This can be a significant problem in academic literature reviews, where accuracy and reliability are crucial. Agentic AI, which refers to AI systems that can act autonomously and make decisions, can help to mitigate hallucinations by providing a more nuanced and context-dependent understanding of the text (Clark, 2013).

The use of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews can also help to improve the overall quality of the review. For example, it can help to identify and address biases in the literature, and provide a more comprehensive and systematic review of the existing research (Page et al., 2021). Additionally, Agentic AI can help to facilitate the use of systematic review methods, such as those outlined in the PRISMA 2020 guidelines (Page et al., 2021).

However, the use of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews also raises several challenges and concerns. For example, there is a need for more research on the potential benefits and limitations of using Agentic AI in this context (Dwivedi et al., 2023). Furthermore, there is a need for more guidance on how to evaluate and validate the results of Agentic AI-powered literature reviews (Feuerriegel et al., 2023).

In conclusion, the integration of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews has the potential to mitigate LLM hallucinations and improve the overall quality of the review. However, more research is needed to fully understand the benefits and limitations of using Agentic AI in this context.

References:
Clark, A. (2013)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., & Hughes, L. (2023)
Feuerriegel, S., Hartmann, J., & Janiesch, C. (2023)
Page, M. J., Moher, D., & Bossuyt, P. M. (2021)
